working_definition: A multipotent early dermal progenitor fibroblast located in the upper dermis of prenatal skin and hair-bearing skin organoid (SkO), abundant before 11 PCW, defined by expression of the HOX transcription factor HOXC5. Gives rise to both a hair fibroblast trajectory (pre-dermal condensate, dermal condensate, dermal papilla) and a dermal fibroblast trajectory (WNT2+ and PEAR1+ fibroblasts).

best_match.cl_definition: Any skin fibroblast that is part of some dermis.

justification: CL:0002551 (fibroblast of dermis) is the closest available CL term for this upper-dermal progenitor population. No CL term exists for an early or HOXC5-expressing progenitor fibroblast. CL:0020011 (hair follicle associated fibroblast) covers only one of the two trajectories produced by this progenitor; the broad dermal fibroblast term better represents the undifferentiated progenitor state.

other_candidates.cl_definition: A connective tissue cell which secretes an extracellular matrix rich in collagen and other macromolecules.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Too general; no skin or dermis specificity.
Less specific than fibroblast of dermis; CL:0002551 is a direct child and better captures the dermal location.

other_candidates.cl_definition: A specialized dermal fibroblast within the hair follicle mesenchymal compartment that participates in epithelial-mesenchymal signaling to regulate hair morphogenesis, cycling, and homeostasis through production of inductive signals and extracellular matrix maintenance.
other_candidates.reason_rejected: Captures only the hair follicle lineage; HOXC5+ cells are an upstream progenitor that also produces non-hair-associated (WNT2+, PEAR1+) dermal fibroblasts.

recommendation: Use CL:0002551 (fibroblast of dermis) as a broad match. A new CL term for an early multipotent upper-dermal progenitor fibroblast (HOXC5+) would be biologically justified given its distinct progenitor identity and dual trajectory output, but should await further cross-dataset validation.
